hero.question: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by April 15?
hero.prob_take: The crowd has essentially priced out a near-term Iran-Israel ceasefire, and they're probably right: 5.1% odds reflect the baseline reality that regional conflicts rarely de-escalate in 45 days once kinetic ops are live. The real question is whether escalation risk (wider US involvement, Iranian retaliation cycles) gets repriced higher before April 15, which would make YES even cheaper and worth nibbling.

daily_take.headline: Iran Conflict Resolution Frozen at 5%: The Market's Calling Your Bluff
daily_take.deck: The Iran-Israel conflict market hasn't budged in 24 hours despite persistent regional tensions and diplomatic chatter. At 5.1% for resolution by April 15, smart money is either convinced this drags past spring or sees a mispriced tail risk. Three months is actually enough time for a negotiated pause, yet the market prices it like war is destiny. Contrarian play: watch for any ceasefire framework announcement, this implodes to 15%+ overnight.
